getting sick and but if you're wearing it out in the supermarket believing that the air that you're breathing in is going to be somehow filtered what you're wasting your time and I think that's where people get confused this is not an air filter it's a barrier that's the best way to think about it does a degree of filtering like anything but think of it more as a barrier and that also depends on how you think these viruses are spread and this is where there's another possibly false dichotomy which is they are described as either airborne or droplets bread and droplets are basically visible drops of that come out of your mouth when you cough and you can see them go whew and they may be blown a little bit but they think they're about it 1 to 2 meters that they can spread and then aerosols so some viruses do live a very well-known viruses and live in basically live in the air and floats around for hours probably the best example is measles so measles can float in the air for many hours if you get into a lift after an elevator after someone's gone in there an hour afterwards you can get sick which is why the average person who
